title: Kenyan Meat Dumplings
description: A traditional Kenyan dish made with juicy meat and flavorful spices, served in a steamed dumpling. Perfect for a hearty meal.
instructions: In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the tomato paste, curry powder, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the filling is cooking, mix the flour and a pinch of salt in a large bowl.
Add the warm water to the flour mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each one into a ball.
Flatten each ball of dough into a disk and place a tablespoon of the meat filling in the center.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Place the dumplings in a steamer basket, leaving a little space between each one.
Steam the dumplings over boiling water for 15-20 minutes, until cooked through.
